title: centaurea antennata subsp. meridionalis
intro: Centaurea antennata subsp. meridionalis, also known as the Southern Mountain Knapweed, is a plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its tall stature, purple flowers, and a distribution mainly in the mountains of the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of Centaurea antennata subsp. meridionalis include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its use in the treatment of various health conditions. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it accessible for those seeking its potential health benefits.
